James A. Baker, representing Brit
fish Columbia on the executive board
Of the Western Federation of Miners,
arrived in the city last night. Mr..
Baker has been in Denver in active
work on the board since May IL. The
Citizens alliance has ordered that we
can not even furnish food for the
families of the miners whom they
have, with the assistance of the
militia, run out of the state. or de-
ported, as they call it.


I'VE sent money to the credit Of
these people to merchants who had
been identified with neither our
unions nor those of the other organi-
cation. Their credit good, the mother.
the wife and the baby were still re-
fused food because the merchant
who had the money was forbidden to
sell goods to our people. We then
resorted to the mails and sent money
orders to members Of the woman's
auxiliary, but they were spotted and
in less than an hour after the order
was recorded in the postoffice the
